title: Gaultheria sulawesiensis
taxonomy: Gaultheria sulawesiensis is classified within the family Ericaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the heath family. This family consists of over 4,000 species of plants, many of which are shrubs or trees. Gaultheria sulawesiensis is specifically classified within the genus Gaultheria, a group of evergreen shrubs native to the Asia-Pacific region. The genus Gaultheria is known for its small, white flowers and red or purple berries. Gaultheria sulawesiensis is also known to be a species of blueberry shrub native to Sulawesi, an island in Indonesia.
